children.heading: Establishment
children.label: a Establishment
children.text: There is established a commission to be known as the Indian Law and Order Commission (referred to in this section as the “Commission”).

children.label: i
children.text: the Attorney General; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the Secretary;

children.label: B
children.text: 2 shall be appointed by the Majority Leader of the Senate, in consultation with the Chairpersons of the Committees on Indian Affairs and the Judiciary of the Senate;

children.label: C
children.text: 1 shall be appointed by the Minority Leader of the Senate, in consultation with the Vice Chairperson and Ranking Member of the Committees on Indian Affairs and the Judiciary of the Senate;

children.label: D
children.text: 2 shall be appointed by the Speaker of the House of Representatives, in consultation with the Chairpersons of the Committees on the Judiciary and Natural Resources of the House of Representatives; and

children.label: E
children.text: 1 shall be appointed by the Minority Leader of the House of Representatives, in consultation with the Ranking Members of the Committees on the Judiciary and Natural Resources of the House of Representatives.

children.label: A
children.text: the Indian country criminal justice system; and

children.label: B
children.text: matters to be studied by the Commission.

children.heading: Consultation required
children.label: 3 Consultation required
children.text: The President, the Speaker and Minority Leader of the House of Representatives, and the Majority Leader and Minority Leader of the Senate shall consult before the appointment of members of the Commission under paragraph (1) to achieve, to the maximum extent practicable, fair and equitable representation of various points of view with respect to the matters to be studied by the Commission.

children.heading: Term
children.label: 4 Term
children.text: Each member shall be appointed for the life of the Commission.

children.heading: Time for initial appointments
children.label: 5 Time for initial appointments
children.text: The appointment of the members of the Commission shall be made not later than 60 days after the date of enactment of this Act.

children.label: A
children.text: in the same manner in which the original appointment was made; and

children.label: B
children.text: not later than 60 days after the date on which the vacancy occurred.

children.heading: Chairperson
children.label: 1 Chairperson
children.text: Not later than 15 days after the date on which all members of the Commission have been appointed, the Commission shall select 1 member to serve as Chairperson of the Commission.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Commission shall meet at the call of the Chairperson.

children.heading: Initial meeting
children.label: B Initial meeting
children.text: The initial meeting shall take place not later than 30 days after the date described in paragraph (1).

children.heading: Quorum
children.label: 3 Quorum
children.text: A majority of the members of the Commission shall constitute a quorum, but a lesser number of members may hold hearings.

children.heading: Rules
children.label: 4 Rules
children.text: The Commission may establish, by majority vote, any rules for the conduct of Commission business, in accordance with this chapter and other applicable law.

children.heading: Comprehensive study of criminal justice system relating to Indian country
children.label: d Comprehensive study of criminal justice system relating to Indian country
A
children.text: the investigation and prosecution of Indian country crimes; and

children.label: B
children.text: residents of Indian land;

children.label: A
children.text: reducing Indian country crime; and

children.label: B
children.text: rehabilitation of offenders;

children.label: A
children.text: tribal juvenile justice systems and the Federal juvenile justice system as relating to Indian country; and

children.label: B
children.text: the effect of those systems and related programs in preventing juvenile crime, rehabilitating Indian youth in custody, and reducing recidivism among Indian youth;

children.label: A
children.text: the authority of Indian tribes;

children.label: B
children.text: the rights of defendants subject to tribal government authority; and

children.label: C
children.text: the fairness and effectiveness of tribal criminal systems; and
studies of such other subjects as the Commission determines relevant to achieve the purposes of the Tribal Law and Order Act of 2010.
simplifying jurisdiction in Indian country;

children.label: A
children.text: to prevent juvenile crime on Indian land;

children.label: B
children.text: to rehabilitate Indian youth in custody; and

children.label: C
children.text: to reduce recidivism among Indian youth;
adjustments to the penal authority of tribal courts and exploring alternatives to incarceration;
the enhanced use of chapter 43 of title 28 (commonly known as “the Federal Magistrates Act”) in Indian country;
effective means of protecting the rights of victims and defendants in tribal criminal justice systems (including defendants incarcerated for a period of less than 1 year);
changes to the tribal jails and Federal prison systems; and
other issues that, as determined by the Commission, would reduce violent crime in Indian country.
a detailed statement of the findings and conclusions of the Commission; and
the recommendations of the Commission for such legislative and administrative actions as the Commission considers to be appropriate.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Commission may hold such hearings, meet and act at such times and places, take such testimony, and receive such evidence as the Commission considers to be advisable to carry out the duties of the Commission under this section.

children.heading: Public requirement
children.label: B Public requirement
children.text: The hearings of the Commission under this paragraph shall be open to the public.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: A witness requested to appear before the Commission shall be paid the same fees and allowances as are paid to witnesses under .section 1821 of title 28

children.heading: Per diem and mileage
children.label: B Per diem and mileage
children.text: The fees and allowances for a witness shall be paid from funds made available to the Commission.

children.heading: Information from Federal, tribal, and State agencies
children.label: 3 Information from Federal, tribal, and State agencies

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Commission may secure directly from a Federal agency such information as the Commission considers to be necessary to carry out this section.

children.heading: Tribal and State agencies
children.label: B Tribal and State agencies
children.text: The Commission may request the head of any tribal or State agency to provide to the Commission such information as the Commission considers to be necessary to carry out this section.

children.heading: Postal services
children.label: 4 Postal services
children.text: The Commission may use the United States mails in the same manner and under the same conditions as other agencies of the Federal Government.

children.heading: Gifts
children.label: 5 Gifts
children.text: The Commission may accept, use, and dispose of gifts or donations of services or property.

children.heading: Travel expenses
children.label: 1 Travel expenses
children.text: A member of the Commission shall be allowed travel expenses, including per diem in lieu of subsistence, at rates authorized for an employee of an agency under subchapter I of chapter 57 of title 5, while away from the home or regular place of business of the member in the performance of the duties of the Commission.

children.heading: Detail of Federal employees
children.label: 2 Detail of Federal employees
children.text: On the affirmative vote of ⅔ of the members of the Commission and the approval of the appropriate Federal agency head, an employee of the Federal Government may be detailed to the Commission without reimbursement, and such detail shall be without interruption or loss of civil service status, benefits, or privileges.

children.heading: Procurement of temporary and intermittent services
children.label: 3 Procurement of temporary and intermittent services
children.text: On request of the Commission, the Attorney General shall provide to the Commission, on a reimbursable basis, reasonable and appropriate office space, supplies, and administrative assistance.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: On an affirmative vote of ⅔ of the members of the Commission, the Commission may select nongovernmental researchers and experts to assist the Commission in carrying out the duties of the Commission under this section.

children.heading: National Institute of Justice
children.label: B National Institute of Justice
children.text: The National Institute of Justice may enter into a contract with the researchers and experts selected by the Commission under subparagraph (A) to provide funding in exchange for the services of the researchers and experts.

children.heading: Other organizations
children.label: 2 Other organizations
children.text: Nothing in this subsection limits the ability of the Commission to enter into contracts with any other entity or organization to carry out research necessary to carry out the duties of the Commission under this section.

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: 1 Establishment
children.text: The Commission shall establish a committee, to be known as the “Tribal Advisory Committee”.

children.heading: Composition
children.label: A Composition
children.text: The Tribal Advisory Committee shall consist of 2 representatives of Indian tribes from each region of the Bureau of Indian Affairs.

children.label: i
children.text: justice systems;

children.label: ii
children.text: crime prevention; or

children.label: iii
children.text: victim services.

children.label: A
children.text: serve as an advisory body to the Commission; and

children.label: B
children.text: provide to the Commission advice and recommendations, submit materials, documents, testimony, and such other information as the Commission determines to be necessary to carry out the duties of the Commission under this section.

children.heading: Funding
children.label: k Funding
children.text: For the fiscal year after , out of any unobligated amounts available to the Secretary of the Interior or the Attorney General, the Secretary or the Attorney General may use to carry out this section not more than $2,000,000.July 29, 2010

children.heading: Termination of Commission
children.label: l Termination of Commission
children.text: The Commission shall terminate 90 days after the date on which the Commission submits the report of the Commission under subsection (f).

children.heading: Nonapplicability of chapter 10 of title 5
children.label: m Nonapplicability of chapter 10 of title 5
children.text: Chapter 10 of title 5 shall not apply to the Commission.
